title: Technical Breakdown: addisonbeck.com MVP

ast.value: reference technical-breakdown web personal-site rust astro public

ast: The addisonbeck.com site renders Addison's org-roam second brain as a public-facing static web reader. All content originates from org-roam nodes tagged

. A Rust binary transforms org-element AST JSON into HTML fragments; Astro assembles those fragments into full pages with routing, backlinks, feed, and search. The site is deployed as static HTML via rsync to a Mail-in-a-Box server.

Two-phase build pipeline: a domain-specific Rust renderer handles the hard, bespoke AST transformation (~20 element types, recursive); Astro handles the UI shell (routing, templates, islands). The renderer and the frontend are cleanly separated by a file-based contract (
). Svelte islands provide interactivity (backlink panel, search) without shipping JavaScript to the full document.
independently. The renderer reads this directory; the renderer has no knowledge of how it is produced.
Mail-in-a-Box server: rsync target. nginx serves
as static files. Let's Encrypt cert management is unchanged.
GitHub Actions: no third-party hosting abstractions. Full pipeline is owned.
-tagged org-roam nodes as browsable HTML pages

h1-h6 (level clamped to 6); title is always an array -- render recursively; todo-keyword is always null in the exported AST (TODO/DONE state appears as a prefix string within the title array and renders naturally as part of heading text)
p; trim trailing newline from children
pre/code with language class; content in props.value; HTML-escape value
ul or ol based on props.type ("unordered"/"ordered")
plain _text (NOT sub -- almost always underscore false positive)
internal org-roam link; resolves via slug map to /<slug>
external link using raw-link (NOT path, which strips scheme)
plain text (local paths must not become web links)
Link children may contain inline elements (e.g. link > verbatim) -- always render children recursively.
Catch-all: Unknown types render children transparently and emit debug HTML comment.
and a slug lookup map, returns an HTML
. This is the core rendering logic.
mapping node UUID to canonical slug. Used to resolve id: links to URLs.
Pattern-match on Node variant. For container types (Section, Paragraph, etc.): recursively call render
on each child, join results, wrap in HTML tag. For leaf types (Verbatim, Code, SrcBlock): read value from props, HTML-escape, wrap. For Link: dispatch on link
, resolve internal links via slugs map. For skipped types: return empty string. For unknown: return debug HTML comment.
headline.title is always an array of children -- always render recursively, never use raw-value
paragraph children: trim trailing newline before joining
todo-keyword is always null in exported AST -- ignore it; TODO/DONE state prefix appears as a plain string inside the title array and renders naturally when title children are rendered recursively
Generates URL-safe slugs from node titles and aliases. Builds the slug lookup map used by the renderer for internal link resolution.
lowercases, replaces non-alphanumeric with hyphens, deduplicates hyphens, trims leading/trailing hyphens
maps all titles, aliases, and UUIDs to their canonical node UUID
Slug collision handling: if two nodes produce the same slug from their title, the second is assigned <slug>-<first-8-chars-of-uuid>. Must be deterministic (sort nodes by UUID before processing).
CLI entry point. Parses arguments, drives the full render pipeline.

A single rendered/all.json with embedded HTML strings. Rejected: loads all ~1,940 nodes HTML into memory at once; difficult to debug individual nodes. Accepted: separate files (per
Bundled with nodejs; no extra Nix config; solo-maintainer project
Current latest stable as of late March 2026; reproducibility across all environments
No content silently excluded; renderer stays stateless; nodes sort to feed bottom
Separate HTML fragment files per node
Per-page reads do not load full corpus; individually debuggable; index.json is lightweight manifest
is produced by a separate Emacs-side process and is not the responsibility of this project. (Confidence: High)
All nodes to be published are tagged public in org-roam; the exporter filters to these. (Confidence: High -- confirmed by explore-agent: 4 current nodes, all public-tagged)
The export node JSON schema is stable. (Confidence: High -- confirmed by explore-agent sampling)
AST format is ["element-type", {props-or-null}, ...children] where properties may be null. (Confidence: High -- confirmed by deep-researcher)
The Mail-in-a-Box server requires no nginx config changes for MVP. (Confidence: High)
are already configured. (Confidence: High -- confirmed by explore-agent)

Stage 5 smoke test (Addison verifies before merge):
Node page loads at /<slug> with rendered org content
Backlink panel shows incoming link titles
Feed at / shows nodes sorted by date
Epoch-dated nodes appear at feed bottom without displaying 1970-01-01
Render file link as plain text
Returns plain text, no anchor tag
No critical open questions. All four gap-analysis decisions resolved via ADRs:
Renderer output format: separate HTML fragment files (ADR-044)
Deferred items (intentional scope exclusions, not open questions):
Astro content loader strategy (Astro.glob vs custom collection loader): either works; implementation-plan-maintainer picks the simpler path
No authentication or user data. The site is fully public static HTML. Deploy key stored in GitHub secrets (DEPLOY
); private key never appears in workflow logs. rsync uses SSH.
Rust renderer: emit progress to stderr (node count, any unknown element types encountered). Astro build: standard Astro build output. GitHub Actions: workflow logs capture full build output.
~1,940 nodes at build time. Rust renderer processes each node independently -- O(n) with no cross-node state except slug map. Pagefind chunked WASM index means no large initial download for search.
Build-time scaling only. Renderer is embarrassingly parallel in principle (each node is independent). Pagefind chunked index handles full-text corpus growth. No runtime server to scale.
Initial breakdown -- synthesized from ADR-041 through ADR-044, deep-researcher AST catalog, explore-agent export findings, and architecture memory D6379D36-CF89-4637-934D-CCDDE74D08B3

addisonbeck.com Redesign Architecture and MVP Plan
ADR-041: Use npm as Package Manager for the Astro Project
ADR-042: Pin Rust Toolchain to Stable 1.94.1 via rust-toolchain.toml
ADR-043: Use Epoch Date as LAST
Fallback for Nodes Without This Property
ADR-044: Output Separate HTML Fragment Files Per Node Rather Than a Single JSON Bundle
Explore agent findings: org-roam export structure, worktree state, existing secrets names
Deep researcher findings: complete org-element AST type catalog, link rendering rules, property null handling
